question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/contractController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractLogsById()] Can an unprivileged external user supply index=gt:N&timestamp=eq:T to trigger the validateContractLogsBounds() check that requires primary (timestamp) to have eq when secondary (index) has eq, and then exploit the getContractLogsLowerFilters() path that includes both timestamp.equal and index bounds, causing logs to be returned in an order that misrepresents the event sequence?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/contractController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: extractContractLogsMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply topic0 and topic1 filters simultaneously to cause both IN-clause conditions to be ANDed together, but with the topic normalization stripping leading zeros differently for each, causing asymmetric matching that includes some logs and excludes others, distorting the event history?
